Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Input Data: Format: CSV-like text with Timestamp, Action Type, App, Window, and optional details (Keys, Direction, From/To, etc.). Time Span: 2024-12-11 08:59:50 to 09:08:15 (approx. 8.5 minutes). Applications: Microsoft Word, Google Chrome, Adobe Acrobat, Microsoft Excel. Windows/Documents: Quarterly_Report.docx, Document1.docx, Email - Inbox, Report_Draft.pdf, Budget_2024.xlsx. Actions: FOCUS, TYPING, SAVE, SWITCH, CLICK, SCROLL, HIGHLIGHT, CLOSE.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Option A: Single Case. Treat the whole 8-minute session as one case (Morning Work Session). Option B: Document-Centric. Each document represents a case. Case 1: Document1.docx (Drafting content). Case 2: Email - Inbox (Handling correspondence). Case 3: Report_Draft.pdf (Reviewing). Case 4: Budget_2024.xlsx (Updating data). Case 5: Quarterly_Report.docx (Finalizing summary). Option C: Task-Centric. Group events by logical work units.

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Decision: Option C (Task-Centric) is the most robust for process mining. Refined Logic: Group events by the specific document/window being actively worked on. Case IDs: Case_01_Doc1, Case_02_Email, Case_03_PDF, Case_04_Excel, Case_05_Doc1_Return, Case_06_Quarterly. Better Logic for Process Mining: Document/Task as the Case ID. Case ID: DOC_Document1, EMAIL_AnnualMeeting, PDF_ReportDraft, XLS_Budget2024, DOC_QuarterlyReport.

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait, looking closer: Document1.docx is worked on, then left for Email, then Excel, then returned to Document1.docx. Correction: The user switches between these. Revised Decision: Define the Case ID based on the Window/Document Name being actively manipulated. If the user returns to Document1.docx later, it belongs to the same Case ID (Case_Document1).

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Handling Switches: Assign the Switch event to the Case of the Destination (as it initiates the new work). Self-Correction on Switch Events: Assign to Destination Case (Start of that task). Reviewing the Case Logic: If I split by Document, I get 5 distinct processes. Document1 as one case, it spans 09:00 to 09:07. This is the most analyst-friendly way.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Refining Activity Names: FOCUS -> Activate Window, TYPING -> Edit Content, SAVE -> Save Document, SWITCH -> Switch Context, CLICK -> Interact UI, SCROLL -> Review Content, HIGHLIGHT -> Annotate, CLOSE -> Close Window. For CLICK: Open Email about Annual Meeting -> Open Email, Reply to Email -> Start Reply, Send Email -> Send Email.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Mapping Events: Rows 1: Quarterly_Report Case_QR Activate Window. Rows 2-5: Document1 Case_Doc1 Activate Edit Edit Save. Row 6 SWITCH to Case_Email Enter Context. Rows 7-11 Email Case_Email Open Email Review Compose Reply Edit Reply Send. And so on for PDF Excel Doc1 return QR.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Prompt Instruction: Each logical unit of user work (Document/Email) is a Case. Best Practice for User Activity Mining: Document is the Case ID. Final Plan: Case IDs Case_Document1, Case_Email, Case_PDF, Case_Excel, Case_QuarterlyReport. Assign SWITCH to Destination Case as Switch Context.
